source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the ‘Log_min_duration_statement’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to '-1' (Disabled)
input_row.description: The log_min_duration_statement flag defines the minimum amount of execution time of a statement in milliseconds where the total duration of the statement is logged. Ensure that log_min_duration_statement is disabled, i.e., a value of -1 is set.
input_row.rationale: Logging SQL statements may include sensitive information that should not be recorded in logs. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances. Impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page. 3. Go to the Configuration card. 4. Under Database flags, check that the value of log_min_duration_statement flag is set to -1. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_min_duration_statement is set to -1. gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json| jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_min_duration_statement")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance where the database flag needs to be enabled. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag log_min_duration_statement from the drop-down menu and set a value of -1. 6. Click Save. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list 2. Configure the log_min_duration_statement flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags log_min_duration_statement=-1 Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default log_min_duration_statement is -1. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-DURATION-STATEMENT Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- MIN-DURATION-STATEMENT Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
To automate the audit for this control, the following steps should be taken:

1. **Enumerate Cloud SQL Instances:** Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud sql instances list` to retrieve a list of Cloud SQL instances in the project.

2. **Filter PostgreSQL Instances:** Ensure that only PostgreSQL instances are considered by checking the database version/type from the instance description.

3. **Check `log_min_duration_statement` Flag for Each Instance:**
   - Use the command `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` to retrieve the configuration details of each PostgreSQL instance.
   - Extract the `databaseFlags` section from the JSON output.
   - Check for the presence of the `log_min_duration_statement` flag, and ensure its value is set to `-1`.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** The `log_min_duration_statement` flag is present and its value is `-1` for the PostgreSQL instance.
   - **Fail:** The `log_min_duration_statement` flag is absent, or its value is not `-1`.

By using these steps, the configuration of the PostgreSQL instances can be audited effectively using the available GCP CLI tools without manual intervention.
step2_review:
---
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can be fully audited using GCP CLI (gcloud) commands and/or GCP REST APIs. There is no need for manual intervention unless the automation flags an issue for further investigation.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capabilities:**  
  - `gcloud sql instances list` and `gcloud sql instances describe` are valid for enumerating and inspecting Cloud SQL instances.
  - The `databaseFlags` field in the instance description contains the relevant flag.
- **Other GCP Tools:**  
  - GCP Config (now called Config Controller) does not natively support this flag as a resource for compliance checks, so CLI/API is the best approach.
  - CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant here.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The steps are technically correct and executable as described.
  - The use of `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` is appropriate for extracting `databaseFlags`.
  - Filtering for PostgreSQL by checking the `databaseVersion` field is correct.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**  
  - The logic is correct: the flag must be present and set to `-1`.
- **Program Name:**  
  - The name `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_disabled` is descriptive, but could be slightly improved for clarity and convention (see below).
- **Minor Omissions:**  
  - The assessment does not specify how to handle multiple flags or multiple databases per instance (though Cloud SQL PostgreSQL only allows one DB engine per instance).
  - The assessment does not mention handling pagination for large numbers of instances, though this is a minor technical detail.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Program Name:**  
  - Suggest: `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_equals_minus1`  
    This is more explicit about the value being checked, which is a best practice for program naming in compliance automation.
- **Additional Considerations:**  
  - **Default Value Handling:**  
    If the `log_min_duration_statement` flag is absent, Cloud SQL uses the PostgreSQL default (which is `-1`). The assessment currently treats absence as "Fail", which is stricter than necessary. The CIS benchmark expects the flag to be set to `-1`, but in practice, absence means default (`-1`). This nuance should be documented and justified in the assessment.
  - **API Alternative:**  
    The same check can be performed using the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances) (`GET https://sqladmin.googleapis.com/v1beta4/projects/{project}/instances/{instance}`), which returns the same data as `gcloud sql instances describe`.
  - **Multi-Project Handling:**  
    If the organization has multiple projects, automation should iterate across all relevant projects.
  - **Output Formatting:**  
    The assessment could specify recommended output formats (e.g., JSON, CSV) for integration with SIEM or compliance dashboards.
  - **Error Handling:**  
    The assessment should mention how to handle API errors, permission issues, or unreachable instances.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP best practices for automated compliance checks.
- Minor improvements are recommended for completeness and clarity, but the core approach is solid.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep as Automated.**
- **Modify** the assessment to:
  - Clarify how to handle the case where the flag is absent (document that the default is `-1` and decide if this is Pass or Fail per organizational policy).
  - Recommend a more explicit program name:  
    `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_min_duration_statement_equals_minus1`
  - Optionally, mention that the Cloud SQL Admin API can be used as an alternative to `gcloud`.
  - Add a note about handling multiple projects and output formatting for integration.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section              | Assessment           | Notes/Recommendations                                                                 |
|----------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE               | Fully automatable with GCP CLI/API                                                    |
| Technical Accuracy   | 9/10                | Correct commands, minor omissions (flag absence, naming)                              |
| Improvements         | See above           | Clarify flag absence, improve naming, mention API, multi-project, output format       |
| Confidence           | HIGH                | Solid technical foundation                                                            |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY              | Keep automated, clarify flag absence, improve naming, document edge cases             |

---
